checks.evidence: Product pages (paygent.co.jp/payment_service/connect/ and /connect/linktype_asp) describe Paygent as a Japanese payment gateway supporting 10+ payment methods including credit card, ATM, convenience store, bank net, carrier billing, etc. One-liner accurately reflects the service.

Support site error codes (P001, P002, P016) reference Merchant ID, Connection ID, Connection Password, and client certificate. PHP SDK (Packagist) requires merchant_id, connect_id, connect_password, pem, crt. IXOPAY adapter config shows merchantId, connectionId, connectionPassword, clientCertificate.

Product page details the sales-gated process: inquiry → hearing/quote → application → screening → contract → development → service start (~6 weeks). Pricing requires inquiry (初期費用・月額固定費用・決済手数料). Support site requires login for certain content. No self-serve signup visible.

Product pages describe Link Type (ASP) and Module Integration Type. Support site shows error codes documenting API parameters, POST-based protocol, Shift-JIS encoding, and client certificate auth. Multiple payment methods confirm broad coverage. IXOPAY and PHP community adapters confirm a documented API exists.
No MCP server found for Paygent in any rendered page. Composio docs page shows general MCP setup but no Paygent integration. No other MCP references found.

Product pages confirm sales-gated process requiring contract (~6 weeks). API exists (confirmed by support site error docs, community SDKs, IXOPAY adapter) but behind login. No self-serve trial. Community PHP/Go libraries exist but API is non-standard (POST, Shift-JIS, custom hash security).
